Co-authored-by: rnwood <1327895+rnwood@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: exclude System.ServiceModel.* from CmdletsLoadContext to fix FaultException type identity conflict

The broad Default.Resolving handler loads System.ServiceModel.Primitives (which
contains FaultException<T>) into CmdletsLoadContext, while Cmdlets.dll (loaded
as a NestedModule into the DEFAULT ALC) resolves it from PS7's installation
directory. The same-version assembly in two different ALCs creates different type
identities, causing `catch (FaultException<OrganizationServiceFault>)` in
SetDataverseEntityMetadataCmdlet to silently miss the thrown exception. Entity
creation always fails with a propagated FaultException.

Fix: exclude System.ServiceModel.* from both the Default.Resolving handler and
CmdletsLoadContext.Load() so all code paths share PS7's copy of these assemblies
from the DEFAULT ALC. Dataverse SDK assemblies (Microsoft.Xrm.Sdk, etc.) are
still isolated in CmdletsLoadContext for Azure Functions compatibility.

Co-authored-by: rnwood <1327895+rnwood@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove System.ServiceModel exclusion from Default.Resolving, keep only in CmdletsLoadContext.Load

The previous commit's System.ServiceModel.* exclusion from Default.Resolving broke
module loading on Ubuntu PS7 builds that do NOT ship WCF assemblies. When PS7
does not have System.ServiceModel.Primitives in its installation, the only path to
find it is Default.Resolving loading it from cmdlets/net8.0/ into CmdletsLoadContext.
Excluding it from Default.Resolving caused FileNotFoundException on every module
import in those environments.

Correct approach:
- Default.Resolving: handles System.ServiceModel.* normally (loads from
  cmdlets/net8.0/ into CmdletsLoadContext when PS7 doesn't have them).
- CmdletsLoadContext.Load(): returns null for System.ServiceModel.*, falling back
  to DEFAULT ALC. This ensures one consistent copy regardless of platform:
  * PS7 ships them (Windows): DEFAULT ALC has PS7's copy; CmdletsLoadContext.Load
    returns null so ServiceClient also uses PS7's copy. TYPE MATCH.
  * PS7 doesn't ship them (Ubuntu): Default.Resolving loaded them into
    CmdletsLoadContext; DEFAULT ALC caches that resolution. CmdletsLoadContext.Load
    returns null so ServiceClient falls back to DEFAULT ALC which returns the
    same CmdletsLoadContext instance. TYPE MATCH.

Co-authored-by: rnwood <1327895+rnwood@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct comment tense in CmdletsLoadContext.Load System.ServiceModel exclusion

Co-authored-by: rnwood <1327895+rnwood@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: preload Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Proxy into DEFAULT ALC at module import for PS type resolution

PS7's type resolver searches AssemblyLoadContext.Default.Assemblies. Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Proxy.dll
(containing WhoAmIRequest, PublishXmlRequest, etc.) was only loaded into CmdletsLoadContext
as an internal dependency of ServiceClient. That loading happens entirely within CmdletsLoadContext
via CmdletsLoadContext.Load(), and never fires Default.Resolving, so the assembly never appears
in AssemblyLoadContext.Default.Assemblies. As a result, PS scripts using:
  New-Object Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Messages.WhoAmIRequest
  [Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Messages.PublishXmlRequest]
would fail with "Cannot find type" on Windows PS latest.

Fix: in OnImport(), after setting up Default.Resolving, explicitly call
AssemblyLoadContext.Default.LoadFromAssemblyPath() for Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Proxy.dll. This
loads it directly into DEFAULT ALC, making its types available to PS's type resolver.

CmdletsLoadContext.Load() now also returns null for Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Proxy so that
ServiceClient's internal usage falls back to the same DEFAULT ALC copy, maintaining a
single type-identity instance (no duplicate-ALC issues for request/response types).

Co-authored-by: rnwood <1327895+rnwood@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add AzureFunctionsCompatibilityTests E2E test demonstrating issue #168 fix

Adds AzureFunctionsCompatibilityTests.cs which simulates the Azure Functions PS 7.4
worker environment by pre-loading Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions v6.0.11
(from Azure Functions Core Tools worker dir) into DEFAULT ALC before importing the
module. This reproduces the exact mechanism of issue #168.

Key facts:
- Azure Functions PS 7.4 worker ships MELA v6.0.11 (AssemblyVersion=6.0.0.0)
- Module requires v8.0.0.0
- Stable module (v2.20.0): Default.Resolving throws FormatException parsing "8.0.13+eba..."
  → returns null → .NET uses DEFAULT ALC v6.x → FileLoadException (0x80131621)
- PR module: strips "+eba..." suffix → parses "8.0.13" → routes to CmdletsLoadContext
  → loads v8.x in isolation → success

Also adds CI workflow step to install Azure Functions Core Tools before E2E tests
so the new test runs automatically in CI.

Demo output (stable vs PR):
  Stable v2.20.0: FAILED: FileLoadException 0x80131621 (issue reproduced)
  PR module:      SUCCESS: Connection established (issue resolved)

Co-authored-by: rnwood <1327895+rnwood@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: allow mcp.sentry.dev in SDK Skill Drift Detector firewall (#43)

* Initial plan

* fix: add mcp.sentry.dev to allowed domains in SDK Skill Drift Detector workflow

The workflow was failing because the project's .mcp.json configures a `sentry`
MCP server at https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp. When Claude Code runs in the agentic
workflow, it reads the project-level .mcp.json and tries to connect to this
server. Since mcp.sentry.dev was not in the firewall's allowed domains list,
the connection was blocked and Claude Code reported:

  ERR_API: MCP server(s) failed to launch: sentry

The "Parse agent logs for step summary" step detected this error and marked the
job as failed — even though the actual work (creating skill drift issues) was
completed successfully.

Fix: Add mcp.sentry.dev to the allowed domains in both the workflow source file
(skill-drift-check.md) and the compiled lock file (skill-drift-check.lock.yml).

commit.message:
Fix dup cascade and EmitBranchCompare transpiler bugs (#155)

* Fix dup cascade and EmitBranchCompare transpiler bugs

Fix two transpiler bugs that caused the climber sample to produce broken
output with garbled sprites and game-stuck behavior:

1. EmitBranchCompare AbsoluteX optimization: When ldelem_u1 loads a value
   via LDA array,X followed by dup+ldc+bne, the optimizer incorrectly
   converted LDA to CMP (comparing against itself). Fix checks instruction
   history to determine if LDA array,X IS the comparison value (not a
   second operand), and emits CMP #constant instead.

2. Dup cascade save/reload: In cascading if-else using dup+ldc+bne/beq
   pattern, after an if-block executes (with JSR calls that modify A),
   A no longer holds the original value for subsequent else-if checks.
   Fix saves A to TEMP_HI after CMP in branch handlers (STA does not
   affect flags on 6502), and reloads it at subsequent dup instructions.
   Uses IsDupCascadeStart() lookahead to distinguish equality cascades
   from button bitmask patterns (dup+ldc+and+brfalse).

Adds climber sample to TranspilerTests.Write with verified snapshot,
and updates Write test to include fami assembly files for extern method
resolution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: sort fami assembly files for deterministic cross-platform order

Directory.GetFiles returns files in platform-dependent order, causing
the climber test to produce different ROM output on macOS vs Windows.
Sort the file paths to ensure consistent ordering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review: fix comment, reset cascade flag, scope fami loading

- Update comment to reference TEMP_HI instead of DUP_TEMP for consistency
- Reset _dupCascadeActive in Pop handler so later unrelated dup sequences
  don't incorrectly trigger cascade save/reload
- Scope fami assembly file loading to only samples that use extern methods
  (climber, fami) to reduce unnecessary IO in the test suite

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update climber.gif screenshot to reflect transpiler fixes

Captures the current dotnes climber output showing correctly rendered
platforms, ladders, and 4 visible sprites. The game level renders
properly after the dup cascade and EmitBranchCompare fixes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stelem_i1 ushort high byte extraction in constant-index path

HandleStelemI1's constant-index path was blindly using the last ldc value
as the store value, ignoring arithmetic operations. For the pattern
actor_yy_hi[0] = (byte)(floor_yy >> 8), the shift count 8 was being
stored instead of the actual high byte of the ushort local.

Added targeted detection for ushort high/low byte extraction patterns:
- (byte)(ushort_local >> 8): emit LDA addr+1 (high byte)
- (byte)ushort_local: emit LDA addr (low byte)
Other arithmetic patterns (like scoreboard's BCD digit extraction) are
left unchanged to avoid regressions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix EmitOamSprDecsp4: depth-tracked compound expression parsing

Rewrote the backward argument scan in EmitOamSprDecsp4 to use stack-depth
tracking instead of the previous flat IsConsumedByAdd approach. This correctly
handles compound oam_spr arguments like (byte)(0x30 + (score >> 4)) and
(byte)(0x30 + (score & 0x0f)) that contain Shr/And/Or/Xor binary operators.

Key changes:
- Binary ops at depth 0 enter compound mode (depth=2); nested ops add depth+1
- Value producers (ldc/ldloc) at depth>0 decrement depth (consumed by binop)
- Value producers at depth==0 are standalone arguments
- Added Ldsfld support for static field args (e.g., oam_off)
- Compound args emit inline: LDA local, [binop], CLC, ADC #const
- Moved music BSS variables to avoid overlap with LocalStackBase

This fixes the infinite loop in oam_clear caused by OAM_OFF=6 (not a
multiple of 4), which was caused by the backward scan misinterpreting
shift/mask operands as standalone oam_spr arguments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stelem_i1 ushort high byte extraction for variable-index path

The variable-index path in HandleStelemI1 was missing Shr/Shr_un handling,
so actor_yy_hi[next_actor] = (byte)(floor_yy >> 8) stored the LOW byte
instead of the high byte. This caused actors 1-7 to have yy_hi values
matching their yy_lo values, making them appear at impossibly high world
Y positions and fail the on-screen check.

Added hasShr/shrValue tracking and ushort high byte extraction to the
variable-index code path, matching the fix already in the constant-index
path. Also handles general right shifts via LSR.

Result: all 18 sprites now render (4 actors x 4 sprites + 2 score digits).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: scope dup cascade save, add compound Mul/Sub emission

- Introduced _dupPendingSave flag so STA TEMP_HI only fires on the branch
  immediately following a dup+ldc compare, not on unrelated bne/beq inside
  the if-body. The dup handler sets the flag, the branch handler consumes it.
- Added Mul (via ASL shifts) and Sub (via SEC/SBC) to compound oam_spr
  argument emission. Added explicit throw for unsupported Div/Rem ops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: per-database reconciliation locking with PostgreSQL advisory locks (#19)

* Initial plan

* feat: per-database reconciliation locking and PostgreSQL advisory locks (#6)

Add two layers of serialization to prevent concurrent reconcile operations
against the same database target:

1. In-process lock (tokio Mutex in OperatorContext) for same-replica safety
2. PostgreSQL advisory lock (pg_try_advisory_lock) for cross-replica safety

Lock contention results in controlled requeue with jitter (10-30s) rather
than hard failure. All lock acquisition/release events are observable via
structured tracing logs.

Adds 14 new unit tests covering:
- In-memory lock acquisition, contention, release, and concurrent access
- Advisory lock key derivation (deterministic, collision-resistant)
- Lock contention error variant and display
- Requeue jitter behavior
- Contention delay constant validation
